summary: guard --help still speaks shared/surface (runtime is note/warn/block) and verbs' help cite the deleted specs/architecture/* paths — user-facing strings, outside CITE-RETAG's comment scope

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: clap help/about strings: `guard --help` still speaks shared/surface, but the runtime migrated to the block/warn/note enforcement vocabulary — recut the help to it. Most verbs' help cite the removed specs/architecture/* layout; retag to the recut specs/** layout. These are user-facing strings, distinct from CITE-RETAG's comment scope.

tests.asserts: no help/about string speaks shared/surface or cites a specs/architecture/* path; guard --help names block/warn/note

acceptance: No user-facing help string speaks shared/surface or cites a specs/architecture/* path; guard --help names block/warn/note.
notes: Field report 1. Explicitly outside CITE-RETAG (comment/citation scope, already shipped): these are user-facing strings. Runtime already migrated shared/surface -> note/warn/block.

summary: scaffolded skill modules omit required Skill.description (fails tsc) and install pins SDK ^0.0.2 — caret pins the patch to 0.0.2, which predates the `file` export the scaffold imports

edit.description: Scaffold template: carry the skill's `description` into scaffolded skill modules — Skill.description is required in the SDK types, so omitting it fails tsc (the advertised keystroke wall) even though the runtime erases types. Bump SDK_VERSION_RANGE off `^0.0.2` (:56) to the seam the binary speaks: caret on 0.0.x pins the PATCH (>=0.0.2 <0.0.3), so fresh installs get 0.0.2, which predates the `file` export the scaffold imports; 0.0.4 (seam v2, has `file`) is published. Scaffold output and version range are one contract.

tests.asserts: scaffolded skill modules include description; the scaffolded module + version range import a seam that exports `file`

acceptance: Scaffolded modules typecheck (skill modules carry description) and import an SDK seam that exports `file`; install pins a range that resolves to it.
notes: Field reports T6 + finding 3. blockedBy WIN-INSTALL-SPAWN (same file). Supersedes PACKAGING's 'release-owned bump' framing: the range is a live install-correctness bug fixed here; release owns only future re-pins.

summary: explain prints not-found (retired `import` verb) then narrates the member anyway, and misreports a locked requirement's fill status — the resolver checks one namespace before it finds the member

edit.description: The member resolver prints the not-found error (suggesting the retired `import` verb) then narrates the member anyway — it checks one namespace before the one the resolver eventually finds it in; resolve before the not-found check and drop the retired-verb suggestion. explain <requirement> reports 'required, and unfilled — which check reports as an error' even with a satisfier locked and check reporting nothing; read the lock's requirement/satisfies declarations so explain narrates fill status over the same resolved edges the gate uses.

tests.asserts: explain <member> resolves without a spurious not-found and never suggests the retired `import` verb; explain <requirement> reports fill status agreeing with the gate

acceptance: explain <member> resolves without a spurious not-found; explain <requirement> reports fill status agreeing with the gate; no output suggests the retired `import` verb.
notes: Field report 2 T2 + report 1 finding 7. blockedBy REQUIREMENT-GATE so explain's requirement verdict matches what check now evaluates (Read verbs project over the same resolved edges the gate uses).

summary: on Windows scaffolded modules and lock rows embed backslashes (file("../.claude/rules\\x.md"), mixed-sep source_path) — .temper/ is committed and cross-platform; normalize both writes to /

edit.description: The scaffold writer emits host-separator paths in `file(...)` directives (`file("../.claude/rules\\cls.md")` on Windows); emit `/`-separated paths regardless of host separator.

Lock provenance rows record member source paths with the host separator (mixed-sep source_path on Windows); normalize recorded source paths to `/` so the committed .temper/ artifacts are byte-stable across platforms. (If the source-path row is written elsewhere in the emit path, normalize at that writer.)

tests.asserts: scaffolded file(...) paths and recorded source paths use `/` on every host

acceptance: Scaffolded module paths and lock source_path rows use `/` on every host; committed .temper/ round-trips byte-identically across platforms.
notes: Field report 1. .temper/ is committed and must be cross-platform; a `\`-bearing lock row or scaffold path forks by host. blockedBy SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (same install.rs file).

summary: the PreToolUse guard regex-matches any .claude/ file_path without reading the lock; for file()-carried members the .claude/ file IS the authored source, so block denies editing the source of truth

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: guard() matches GUARD_PATH_MATCH (:104) against any `.claude/` file_path without consulting the lock. For a file()-carried member the `.claude/` file IS its authored source (install already skips placements on such members; emit adopts their direct edits), so under `block` the guard denies editing the source of truth. Read the lock's owned-source paths so a write to an own_path target passes through, while writes to projected `.claude/` files stay bound.
Read `owns_source` (already present) to identify a member's own authored path when the guard consults the lock — read-only from the guard's side.

tests.asserts: guard passes a write to a file()-carried member's own .claude/ source (own_path) and still binds writes to projected .claude/ files

acceptance: The guard passes a write to a file()-carried member's own .claude/ source (own_path) and continues to bind writes to projected .claude/ files, reading the lock rather than a path regex alone.
notes: Field report 1. For file()-carried members the .claude/ file is the source of truth, not a projection; the guard must read the lock's owned-source paths. blockedBy PATH-SEP-NORMALIZE (same install.rs file).

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.4, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.4); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs `^0.0.2` range-correctness now owned by SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (a live install bug); release owns only future re-pins.
